summary: Blueprint is Palantir's open-source React-based UI toolkit for building complex, data-dense desktop web interfaces. It is a general-purpose component library (buttons, forms, dialogs, tables, selects, date pickers, trees, etc.) with no Ontology-specific functionality. The monorepo publishes packages under the @blueprintjs scope. The only Ontology-adjacent content is icon tag metadata referencing 'ontology' as a search keyword for relationship icons (one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-one, many-to-many).

apiSurface.description: Core UI primitives: buttons, cards, dialogs, forms, menus, overlays, tabs, toasts, tooltips, trees, progress bars, spinners, and layout components.

Scalable interactive spreadsheet-like table component with cell renderers, column headers, row resizing, and region selection. Could be used to display tabular Ontology data but contains no Ontology awareness.
Item selection components (Select, MultiSelect, Suggest, Omnibar) with filtering, custom renderers, and tag inputs. Generic list selection with no Ontology typing.
Date and time picker components (DatePicker, DateRangePicker, TimePicker).
Icon set with SVG and icon font implementations. Contains relationship icons (one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-one, many-to-many) tagged with 'ontology' keyword for discoverability.
Experimental layout primitives (Box, Flex, Slot). No Ontology components.

architecturePatterns.description: Standard React component architecture with props-based API, controlled/uncontrolled patterns, and render prop callbacks for customization.
pnpm workspaces with Nx task orchestration and Lerna-Lite for releases. Packages in packages/ directory.
CSS class-based styling with Sass variables. Light and dark theme support via CSS classes.
